description: Letter in Judaeo-Arabic to a notable (yeḥid ha-dor, sayyidnā), possibly named al-Shaykh Yaʿīsh. Reporting on developments in the community after the addressee (or another 'sayyidnā') departed. Involves Abū l-Faraj and a troublemaker who said, "You shut up!" And he won't let anyone say a word to him. He is also going between the two factions in the community and making up stories about the other faction. There is not a day without people coming to blows ("to death"). The writer asks the addressee to please advise. Reused on recto for Hebrew writing practice.
description_he: מכתב בHebrew, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
